steps.title: Identify the human tendency
steps.content: The narrator says people find nuance troublesome and prefer deciding that a neighbor is good for nothing. That comment explains why the people of Rotherby might hold an exaggeratedly negative view of the Countess.

steps.title: Avoid unsupported aims
steps.content: Choice B focuses on improving the opinion, Choice C says the reproach is justified, and Choice D claims the text details multiple viewpoints. The passage instead comments on quick judgment. The answer is A.
